full_response:
Step 1.  Set-up and notation  

• Work in flat space–time (special relativity, c = 1).  
• Rocket points in the +x–direction and is instantly at rest in some inertial frame.  
• Proper length of the rocket (measured in that instantaneous rest frame) is  
  L = 0.5 ly.  
• Proper acceleration measured at the tip (the “top”) is  
  α_top = 1 g ≃ 1 ly yr⁻² = 1 ly⁻¹ (because c = 1 ⇒ 1 yr = 1 ly).  
• We want the proper acceleration α_bottom felt at the tail (“bottom”).

Step 2.  Born-rigid acceleration field (Rindler coordinates)  

For a body that remains Born-rigid while accelerating along x, every point of the body follows a different hyperbolic world-line.  A convenient parametrisation is the Rindler chart

  T = ρ sinh η ,  X = ρ cosh η ,  ρ > 0,  –∞ < η < ∞,

whose metric is ds² = –ρ² dη² + dρ² + dy² + dz² .

A world-line at fixed ρ has

 Proper acceleration α(ρ) = 1 / ρ.                (1)

(The 4-acceleration magnitude of a hyperbola X² – T² = ρ² is 1/ρ.)

Step 3.  Relating the two ends of the rocket  

At one instant of Rindler time η the spatial line element is simply dℓ² = dρ², so the proper distance between two points of fixed ρ is just their coordinate difference.  Therefore

  L = ρ_top – ρ_bottom.                           (2)

From eq. (1) the top world-line (where the astronaut is sitting) satisfies

  ρ_top = 1 / α_top.                              (3)

Combining (2) and (3):

  ρ_bottom = ρ_top – L = 1/α_top – L.            (4)

Step 4.  Proper acceleration at the bottom  

Insert (4) into (1):

  α_bottom = 1 / ρ_bottom  
       = 1 / (1/α_top – L)  
       = α_top / (1 – α_top L).                (5)

Step 5.  Numerical evaluation  

α_top = 1 g = 1 ly yr⁻² = 1 ly⁻¹, L = 0.5 ly.

α_top L = 1 ly⁻¹ × 0.5 ly = 0.5, so

  α_bottom = 1 ly⁻¹ / (1 – 0.5) = 1 ly⁻¹ / 0.5 = 2 ly⁻¹  
       ≃ 2 g.

(If α_top L were ≥ 1 the bottom would lie beyond the Rindler horizon and the rocket could not remain rigid.)

\boxed{\alpha_{\text{bottom}} = 2\,g\;\;(\text{twice the proper acceleration at the top})}

Confidence: 82%
